summary: First testing iteration. Backend: 18/18 pytest tests passed (auth register/login/me, emergency alert/history/active for all types, weather current/forecast/recommendations, profile PUT). Frontend: app loads, register auto-logs-in and lands on Emergency tab, MainTabs navigation works, mode toggle works (Di Rumah <-> Pergi), Weather screen shows all data (temp/humidity/wind/UV/AQI/5-day forecast/Sunscreen-Payung-Masker cards), bilingual ID/EN toggle works, logout menu opens when user pill tapped. ONE critical web-only bug: Alert.alert confirmation dialog for SOS/MEDIS/KRIMINAL/KEBAKARAN does not appear on react-native-web (multi-button Alert is not polyfilled), so emergency alerts cannot be confirmed/sent via web preview - but on real mobile device it would work via native Alert.

minor.issue: If JWT 'sub' is not a valid ObjectId, bson.errors.InvalidId is raised and not caught -> returns 500 instead of 401. Wrap ObjectId() in try/except.
JWT_SECRET_KEY falls back to a hardcoded default ('siskamling-secret-key-change-in-production'). Should be loaded strictly from env (no default) for production safety.
allow_origins=['*'] combined with allow_credentials=True violates CORS spec; browsers will reject credentialed requests. Use explicit origin list when credentials are needed.

ui_bugs.issue: Alert.alert(title, message, [Batal, Kirim]) does NOT render any dialog on react-native-web. Clicking SOS/MEDIS/KRIMINAL/KEBAKARAN on web preview produces zero UI feedback and never calls POST /api/emergency/alert. On native mobile this works, but the deliverable web preview is broken. Use a cross-platform confirm UI (e.g., a Modal component) or Platform.OS === 'web' branch using window.confirm().

No testID attributes on any TouchableOpacity / TextInput / Pressable. Tests must rely on text/placeholder which is fragile (Stack.Navigator keeps Login screen mounted on web, causing duplicate Email/Password placeholders). Add testIDs for SOS button, each emergency type card, mode-toggle, language-toggle, user-pill, logout, login/register submit, all TextInputs.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot be triggered from web preview because confirmation Alert never appears (see ui_bugs). Backend endpoint itself works (verified by pytest).

issues: Console warns: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow"'. Cosmetic only.
Expo log: 'Font file for ionicons is empty' - but icons still render correctly in screenshots (fallback works). May want to verify on real device build.

react_native_issues.issue: Uses Alert.alert with custom buttons - not portable to react-native-web. This is the root cause of the SOS dialog bug.

action_items: Replace Alert.alert(title, msg, [buttons]) in EmergencyScreen.sendEmergencyAlert with a cross-platform confirmation (e.g., a Modal with Batal/Kirim buttons, or branch on Platform.OS === 'web' to use window.confirm). Same applies to the success/failure Alerts which currently silently fail on web.

Add testID props to all interactive elements (SOS button, MEDIS/KRIMINAL/KEBAKARAN cards, mode-toggle, language-toggle, user-pill, logout button, all TextInputs, Masuk, Daftar) to enable robust automated UI testing.
Backend: wrap ObjectId(user_id) in get_current_user with try/except bson.errors.InvalidId -> 401.
Backend: require JWT_SECRET_KEY from env (no default) and document in .env.example.
Backend: tighten CORS allow_origins to the EXPO frontend URL when allow_credentials=True.
critical_code_review_comments: EmergencyScreen.tsx Alert.alert multi-button pattern is broken on react-native-web - critical UX bug for web deliverable.
Login screen remains mounted in DOM after navigating to Register on web (Stack.Navigator behavior), causing duplicate inputs. Consider using a single-screen conditional or testIDs to disambiguate.
No testID coverage anywhere in the app.
JWT secret key fallback is insecure.
ObjectId construction not guarded against invalid input.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (login, register, navigation, tabs, mode toggle, language toggle, weather data, recommendations, forecast, logout menu all working; SOS confirmation dialog broken on web)

seed_data_creation: Created test user budi27106@test.com / password123 (Budi Wijaya, RW 07 RT 03) via UI registration. Backend pytest creates additional TEST_-prefixed users with unique uuids each run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully verified via /app/backend/tests/test_siskamling_api.py (run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v). Frontend test user budi27106@test.com / password123 is already registered. KEY ISSUE: Alert.alert with custom buttons does not work on react-native-web - this affects ALL emergency alert flows on web preview. After E1 fixes by adding a Modal-based confirmation (or Platform.OS web branch), retest emergency alert end-to-end. Also retest after testIDs are added so selectors become reliable.
rca_of_issue: Emergency alert silent failure on web preview: react-native-web's Alert polyfill only supports the single-button form Alert.alert(title, message). When a buttons array is passed (as in sendEmergencyAlert), the polyfill is a no-op - no dialog renders and no onPress fires. Therefore clicking SOS/MEDIS/KRIMINAL/KEBAKARAN on web produces no UI feedback and the POST /api/emergency/alert call is never made. Reproduction: open web preview, login, tap SOS - nothing happens. Mitigation: replace with a cross-platform <Modal> confirmation component, or branch Platform.OS === 'web' to use window.confirm() / a custom modal, while keeping Alert.alert for native iOS/Android.
